case_name: Chase Michael Richard v. Chase Langston Wiatt

summary.brief: Chase Michael Richard v. Chase Langston Wiatt. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: On a Saturday afternoon in September 2016, appellant Chase Richard was stopped in traffic when his vehicle was struck from behind by a car driven by appellee Chase Wiatt.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Witness Dalton Hernandez stated that about a minute before the accident, he had been driving next to Wiatt when Wiatt, who was looking down at his phone, had begun to drift into Hernandez’s lane.
evidence_index.speaker: Witness Dalton Hernandez

evidence_index.text: The evidence at trial included billing records from Memorial Hermann Northeast Hospital, AD Hospital East, Beyer Chiropractic, Advanced Diagnostics, and Progressive Medicine Associates, but no other medical records.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Richard testified, however, that the x-rays and CT scan were normal, and that records from AD Hospital East reported that the accident had no effect on his activities of daily living, his emotions, or his mental status, and that the records further stated his range of motion was painless and within normal limits.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Beyer Chiropractic referred Richard to Elite Health Services for magnetic resonance imaging of Richard’s cervical and lumbar spine. The MRIs revealed five mild disc herniations: two to Richard’s cervical spine and three to his lumbar spine.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court granted Wiatt’s motion for directed verdict on Richard’s gross-negligence claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury found that Richard’s reasonable expenses for necessary medical care in the past was only $15,584.45—exactly half of the amount requested.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Because the evidence is insufficient to support a firm belief or conviction that Wiatt had actual subjective awareness that his conduct made serious injury likely, we conclude that the trial court did not err in granting a directed verdict on Richard’s gross-negligence claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Having overruled both of Richard’s appellate issues, we affirm the trial court’s judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Leg and cervical spine pain after motor vehicle accident
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was involved in a rear-end collision while stopped in traffic. Declined ambulance transport and self-presented to the hospital.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Post-traumatic pain following motor vehicle accident
critical_findings: No abnormal findings on initial imaging

requestables.name: X-ray Leg and Cervical Spine
requestables.description: Initial imaging performed at Memorial Hermann Northeast Hospital

requestables.name: MRI Cervical and Lumbar Spine
requestables.description: Referred by Beyer Chiropractic to Elite Health Services

reveal.result_summary: Five mild disc herniations: two cervical, three lumbar
reveal.clinical_significance: Mild disc herniations noted, no immediate surgical indication

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Additional Imaging
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes further imaging and chiropractic care

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with post-accident pain and normal initial imaging
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Refer to physical therapy for conservative management
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for soft tissue injuries post-accident

options.description: Order additional imaging and chiropractic care
options.clinical_reasoning: Additional imaging not indicated given normal initial findings

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant pursued additional imaging and chiropractic care
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed further imaging was necessary

expected_action_court.description: Referral to physical therapy should have been considered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Additional imaging was not supported by initial findings
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Conservative management should have been prioritized
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for post-accident management

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced ongoing pain but no acute injury identified
